description_long: This object is a group of stylized trees of varying sizes, arranged in a line from tallest to shortest. The tallest tree features a thick, textured trunk with a dense, rounded canopy of layered, broad leaves in shades of pink and brown, accented by occasional hints of green. The smaller trees display similar leaf structures but with more muted gray and green tones, and their trunks are thinner and less prominent. The foliage appears to be constructed from overlapping, flat segments, giving the trees a somewhat abstract, geometric appearance. The overall form suggests deciduous trees, possibly in autumn, with a mix of mature and young specimens. No visible roots extend above the ground, and the trees are not hollow or open.
description: A stylized group of deciduous trees with layered, broad leaves in pink, brown, gray, and green, ranging from tall to short. The trees have textured trunks and dense, rounded canopies.
description_view_0: A side view showing the full lineup of trees from tallest to shortest, highlighting the color gradient and canopy density.
description_view_1: A frontal view of the tallest tree, with its dense pink-brown canopy and thick trunk most prominent, and the smaller trees mostly obscured behind.
description_view_2: The opposite side view, again showing the lineup from smallest to tallest, with the color and size progression reversed.
description_view_3: A rear view focused on the tallest tree, displaying the back of its dense canopy and trunk, with other trees mostly hidden.
